variants.subject: IMN Nashville — 1,200-unit BTR pipeline needs best-in-class operators
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

I'll be at the IMN BTR Spring Conference in Nashville March 16-17 and wanted to connect about property management for our BTR pipeline.

Tikkun is developing 1,219 BTR units across four Florida projects — all factory-built concrete, Section 8 eligible, in high-growth Florida submarkets with 2+ year housing waitlists:

• Port St. Lucie — 250 units
• Modern Towers — 396 units
• Miami Gardens — 500 units
• Infinity Gardens — 73 units

We're looking for operator partners who understand affordable housing compliance, Section 8 administration, and BTR community management. The right partner gets in early on a scalable Florida pipeline.

Would love to discuss what a partnership with {{companyName}} could look like at the Grand Hyatt.

Ryan McFarland
Tikkun Holdings

variants.subject: Concrete BTR communities: the operator advantage
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

Quick context on why these communities are different to operate — in a good way.

Income Stability:
• Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers — government-guaranteed rent
• $2,200-2,400/month per unit
• 98%+ collection rates
• 2+ year waitlists in every submarket we're building in

Lower Operating Costs:
• Concrete construction = minimal structural maintenance
• 30-40% lower insurance costs vs. wood frame
• 75+ year structural life — no wood rot, no termite risk
• Sound insulation between units reduces noise complaints

Resident Quality:
• Section 8 tenants go through federal screening
• Long-term tenancy (average 4+ years)
• Stable community with low turnover

The right operator can maximize NOI on these assets while delivering an exceptional resident experience. Happy to share the pipeline details ahead of Nashville.

Ryan McFarland
Tikkun Holdings

variants.subject: At IMN next week — let's talk Florida BTR operations
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

Conference is next week at the Grand Hyatt. We're bringing the full pipeline — 4 projects, 1,219 units, all in Florida.

If {{companyName}} operates or is expanding into Florida BTR and affordable housing, I'd love to discuss how we could partner on the management side. We want an operator who can grow with the platform.

Would 15 minutes at the conference work?

Ryan McFarland
Tikkun Holdings

variants.subject: Tomorrow at Grand Hyatt — 15 min on the pipeline?
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

We arrive tomorrow for IMN. Happy to walk through the pipeline and discuss what the operator partnership looks like — scope, timeline, and how we structure the relationship.

Feel free to text me at (305) 555-0199 or find me at the conference.

Looking forward to connecting,

Ryan McFarland
Tikkun Holdings

variants.subject: Dollar-for-dollar federal tax credits - affordable housing fund for accredited investors
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

{{personalization}} - that's why I wanted to reach out directly.

Granite Park Capital Affordable Housing Fund II generates Low Income Housing Tax Credits (LIHTC) that work differently than most real estate investments. These are dollar-for-dollar offsets against your federal tax liability - not deductions, actual credits. For a high-income investor in the 37% bracket, the tax benefit alone can represent a significant portion of the investment return, before the preferred return or appreciation.

Fund II targets 12-16% net IRR with a 7% preferred return paid quarterly. Our parent company, Apartment Corp, manages over 13,500 units, and our first fund was fully subscribed at $50M. Fund II is acquiring Section 8 and LIHTC properties nationwide with government-guaranteed income.

Minimum: $250K. Accredited investors only.

Do you have 30 minutes to see how this fits your situation?
Book a call here

Best,
Colby Watkins
Fund Manager - Granite Park Capital
(508) 397-3792 | colby@granitepark.co

variants.subject: Your next high-velocity SKU (no THCa headaches)
variants.body:
Let’s be real — the THCa game is rough right now. Regulations are changing every other week, testing is all over the place, and half the stuff out there is one lab report away from disappearing.

So we decided to build something that actually makes sense:

  • A fully compliant roll using a clean blend of CBD flower + live resin + THCP + terps
  • Hash-hole style finish for that smooth, elevated feel
  • White label + 3PL ready — you focus on the brand, we handle everything else

No more stressing over THCa rules or playing in a shrinking lane.This gives you a product that’s legal, reliable, and still punches above its weight.

For samples and inquiries, please reach out here.

variants.subject: This pre-roll is about to take over 2026
variants.body:
Most compliant pre-rolls… let’s be honest, they’re a letdown. Sure, they pass testing — but they fail the potency check every time.

Ours don’t.

We’re running a CBD + live resin + THCP infused blend that’s clean, compliant, and actually delivers the kind of kick people come back for.

You handle the branding and the look.We’ll handle the manufacturing, packaging, and shipping.

Give your lineup the compliant product your competitors wish they had.

For samples and inquiries, please reach out here.

variants.subject: THCa slowing down? This is the move
variants.body:
The THCa market is packed right now. Margins are dropping, every shelf looks the same, and retailers are worried.

What they do want? Something compliant that actually hits.

That’s exactly what we’re making.

We take premium CBD flower and infuse it with live resin, THCP, and terpenes to create a legal, shelf-stable roll that hits consistently — without the THCa legal headaches.

We handle the manufacturing, compliance, packaging, and fulfillment.You just focus on growing the brand.

Want to check out samples or pricing? Please reach out here.

variants.subject: Most states allow it. Your customers will LOVE it
variants.body:
Why limit your brand to the shrinking number of states that allow THCa?

Our proprietary CBD flower + Live Resin + THCp infused pre-rolls are: 

• Federally compliant hemp products 
• Ship-ready across majority of states 
• Custom-branded and fulfilled under your label

We’ve helped dozens of brands scale their pre-roll and hash hole revenue without worrying about compliance or licensing.

Let’s talk about your custom blend.  Please reach out here.

variants.subject: Act now: the queue is filling up fast
variants.body:
We’re onboarding a limited number of brands per region to avoid market saturation.
If you want a compliant, premium pre-rolls that delivers a real experience —now’s the time to lock it in.

• Fully white labeled
• 3PL fulfillment available
• Legal in most states
• Tested and trusted by national brands

Don’t get left behind in the next crackdown.  For samples and inquiries, please reach out here.

variants.subject: $200M factory, $10M acquisition — BTR platform play
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

I'm reaching out because {{companyName}} invests in real assets with asymmetric upside — and this is one of the most compelling deals I've seen in BTR.

Tikkun is acquiring a $200M modular concrete factory in Pompano Beach, FL for ~$10M in a leveraged buyout. The factory produces hurricane-rated, fire-resistant concrete wall panels and modular units at a fraction of traditional construction costs.

We have a 1,219-unit BTR pipeline across four Florida projects already under contract or in advanced negotiation:

• Port St. Lucie — 250 units (under contract, 120-day option)
• Modern Towers — 396 units
• Miami Gardens — 500 units
• Infinity Gardens — 73 units

Because we own the factory, our margins run 15-20 points above typical BTR developers. We're looking for JV equity partners who want exposure to a vertically integrated, factory-built BTR platform in the fastest-growing state in the country.

Would you have 15 minutes this week or next to hear the thesis?

Colby
Tikkun Holdings

variants.subject: The math on factory-built BTR
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

Following up with the numbers behind our Port St. Lucie project — because the economics are what make this different.

Tikkun Factory-Built BTR (PSL — 250 units):
• Factory build cost: $65-80K per unit
• All-in cost (land, site, soft costs): $180-220K per unit
• Section 8 rent: $2,200-2,400/month
• Gross yield: 12-15%

Typical Stick-Built BTR:
• All-in cost: $280-350K per unit
• Market rent: $1,800-2,200/month
• Gross yield: 7-9%

The factory advantage compounds at every level — lower basis, faster delivery, government-backed rental income, and a concrete product that lasts 75+ years with minimal maintenance.

Happy to walk through the full pro forma if this is in your wheelhouse.

Colby
Tikkun Holdings

variants.subject: 1,200-unit Florida BTR portfolio — factory-built
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

Wanted to highlight the portfolio-level opportunity here — because the individual projects are compelling, but the platform story is even stronger.

Tikkun BTR Pipeline — 1,219 Units Across 4 Florida Projects:

1. Port St. Lucie — 250 units (under contract)
2. Modern Towers — 396 units
3. Miami Gardens — 500 units
4. Infinity Gardens — 73 units

Modern Towers and Miami Gardens share the same landowner — 896 combined units that can be structured as a single deployment. That's a $50-100M deployment opportunity with a single counterparty.

All units are factory-built concrete, Section 8 eligible, and located in high-demand Florida submarkets with 2+ year housing waitlists.

If portfolio-scale BTR is something {{companyName}} is allocating toward, I'd love to share the details.

Colby
Tikkun Holdings

variants.subject: Government-backed BTR — tax credits + guaranteed rent
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

One angle I haven't touched on yet — the government incentive stack on top of the factory cost advantage.

What Tikkun BTR projects qualify for:

• Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers — government-guaranteed rent, 98%+ collection rates, 2+ year waitlists in every FL submarket we're building in
• 4% LIHTC (Low-Income Housing Tax Credits) — as-of-right tax credits that reduce equity basis by 30%+
• Florida Live Local Act — state-level property tax abatement, zoning override for affordable housing
• SAIL (State Apartment Incentive Loan) — low-interest state financing for qualifying projects

These incentives exist for any affordable housing developer. But when you stack them on top of factory build costs that are already 40-50% below conventional construction, the return profile becomes exceptional.

This is institutional-quality impact investing with double-digit yields. Happy to share the subsidy analysis if {{companyName}} has an impact or affordable housing mandate.

Colby
Tikkun Holdings

variants.subject: Closing the loop
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

I've sent a few notes about Tikkun and wanted to close the loop in case the timing isn't right.

Quick summary:
• Acquiring a $200M modular concrete factory in Pompano Beach, FL for ~$10M
• 1,219-unit BTR pipeline across 4 Florida projects
• Factory build costs 40-50% below conventional — gross yields of 12-15%
• Section 8 + LIHTC + Live Local Act incentive stack
• Looking for JV equity partners for a vertically integrated BTR platform

If the timing or fit isn't there right now, no worries at all. But if this crosses your desk again in the future, I'm always happy to pick up the conversation.

Wishing you and the {{companyName}} team a great rest of the quarter.

Colby
Tikkun Holdings

variants.subject: Construction financing for 1,200+ BTR units — factory-advantaged
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

I'm reaching out because {{companyName}} finances multifamily and BTR projects — and our pipeline has a structural advantage that changes the underwriting conversation.

Tikkun is a vertically integrated BTR developer that owns a modular concrete factory in Pompano Beach, FL. We're seeking construction financing for 1,219 BTR units across four Florida projects:

• Port St. Lucie — 250 units
• Modern Towers — 396 units
• Miami Gardens — 500 units
• Infinity Gardens — 73 units

Because we manufacture our own concrete wall panels and modular components, our cost basis runs 40-50% below conventional construction. That translates to lower LTV at the same loan amount, shorter construction timelines (factory production runs parallel to site work), and affordable housing eligibility that unlocks government-backed rental income.

Would {{companyName}} be open to a conversation about how factory-built BTR fits your lending criteria?

Colby
Tikkun Holdings

variants.subject: Why factory-built BTR is a better loan
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

Following up with the lending thesis on factory-built BTR — because from an underwriting perspective, this is a fundamentally different risk profile.

Reduced Construction Risk:
• 60-70% of the building is manufactured off-site in a controlled factory environment
• No weather delays, no labor shortages on the critical path
• Construction timelines compressed by 30-40% vs. conventional

Lower Cost Basis:
• Factory build cost: $65-80K per unit
• All-in: $180-220K per unit vs. $280-350K conventional
• Same loan amount = significantly lower LTV

Income Stability:
• All projects qualify for Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers
• Government-guaranteed rent at $2,200-2,400/month per unit
• 98%+ collection rates, 2+ year waitlists in target submarkets

Superior Asset Quality:
• Concrete construction rated for Category 5 hurricanes
• 2-hour fire rating vs. wood frame
• 75+ year structural life, lower ongoing maintenance and insurance

Happy to share project-level detail if this fits {{companyName}}'s lending appetite.

Colby
Tikkun Holdings

variants.subject: PSL project specifics — 250 BTR units
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

Wanted to share specifics on our most advanced project in case {{companyName}} prefers to evaluate a single deal before looking at the full pipeline.

Port St. Lucie — 250 BTR Units

• Location: Port St. Lucie, FL — one of the fastest-growing MSAs in the U.S.
• Units: 250 single-family and townhome-style BTR
• Status: Under contract with 120-day option period
• Build cost: $65-80K per unit (factory-built concrete panels)
• All-in cost: $180-220K per unit (land, site, soft costs)
• Rental income: Section 8 vouchers at $2,200-2,400/month
• Government programs: 4% LIHTC, Live Local Act, SAIL eligible
• Construction timeline: 12-16 months (vs. 20-24 months conventional)
• Product: Hurricane-rated concrete, 2-hour fire rating, sound-insulated

Entitlements and government program applications are in process. We're looking for a construction lender who understands the factory advantage and wants to be first in on a scalable pipeline.

Would a call make sense to walk through the numbers?

Colby
Tikkun Holdings

variants.subject: Zero tariff exposure on structural components
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

One more angle that's become increasingly relevant given the current trade environment.

Tikkun's factory is in Pompano Beach, FL. Every structural component — concrete wall panels, modular assemblies, MEP cores — is manufactured domestically. That means:

• Zero tariff exposure on structural materials
• No import dependencies for critical path items
• Locked-in production costs — cement, rebar, and aggregate are domestic commodities with stable pricing
• No material price escalation risk during the construction period

For lenders, this eliminates one of the biggest sources of cost overrun risk in conventional construction — and it means our pro formas hold up through delivery.

With lumber tariffs, steel tariffs, and supply chain uncertainty affecting every conventional project in the pipeline, factory-built concrete is the most predictable cost basis in the market right now.

If {{companyName}} is seeing the same dynamics in your construction book, happy to discuss how our projects de-risk differently.

Colby
Tikkun Holdings

variants.subject: Closing the loop on Tikkun
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

I've sent a few notes and want to respect your time — so this will be my last email unless I hear back.

Quick summary of the opportunity:
• 1,219 BTR units across 4 Florida projects seeking construction financing
• Factory-built concrete — 40-50% below conventional cost basis
• Section 8 government-backed rental income
• Domestic manufacturing, zero tariff exposure, locked-in costs
• Compressed construction timelines (12-16 months vs. 20-24 conventional)

If the timing or fit isn't right today, I completely understand. But if {{companyName}} is looking at BTR or affordable housing lending in the future, we'd welcome the conversation.

All the best,

Colby
Tikkun Holdings

variants.subject: Factory-built BTR communities in Florida — operating partner needed
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

I'm reaching out because {{companyName}} operates BTR and multifamily communities — and we're building something in Florida that needs an experienced operating partner.

Tikkun is a vertically integrated BTR developer with our own modular concrete factory in Pompano Beach, FL. We're developing 1,219 BTR units across four Florida projects:

• Port St. Lucie — 250 units
• Modern Towers — 396 units
• Miami Gardens — 500 units
• Infinity Gardens — 73 units

All units are factory-built concrete — hurricane-rated, fire-resistant, sound-insulated — with Section 8 eligibility and government incentive stacking.

We're open to flexible partnership structures: management agreement, operating JV, or acquisition of stabilized communities. Whatever aligns best with how {{companyName}} deploys.

Would you have time for a brief call to explore fit?

Colby
Tikkun Holdings

variants.subject: Why operating Tikkun communities is different
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

Following up — wanted to explain why operating a Tikkun community is a fundamentally different proposition than managing conventional BTR.

Lower Maintenance, Higher NOI:
• Concrete construction = no wood rot, no termite damage, no siding replacement
• Maintenance costs 30-40% lower than wood-frame communities
• 75+ year structural life vs. 30-40 years for stick-built

Superior Resident Experience:
• STC 55+ sound insulation between units (vs. STC 33-38 for wood frame)
• 2-hour fire rating — safest product in the market
• Category 5 hurricane rated — critical for Florida operations

Lower Operating Risk:
• Insurance premiums significantly lower on concrete vs. wood (especially post-Ian in Florida)
• Section 8 eligibility = government-backed rent, 98%+ collection, 2+ year waitlists
• Live Local Act property tax abatement reduces operating expenses

For an operator, this means higher NOI, lower capex reserves, more stable income, and happier residents. If {{companyName}} is evaluating new communities to operate, these are worth a closer look.

Colby
Tikkun Holdings

variants.subject: Three ways to partner with Tikkun
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

Wanted to lay out the partnership structures we're offering — because we know every operator has a different model.

Option 1: Management Agreement
• Tikkun retains ownership, {{companyName}} operates
• Standard fee structure (% of gross revenue + incentive)
• Full pipeline of 1,219 units with ongoing factory production capacity of 2,000 units/year

Option 2: Operating JV
• {{companyName}} co-invests and co-operates
• Shared upside on development margin + stabilized NOI
• Factory cost advantage flows through to the JV

Option 3: Takeout / Acquisition
• Tikkun builds and stabilizes communities
• {{companyName}} acquires stabilized, cash-flowing BTR assets
• Built-in pipeline for repeat transactions — we build, you buy

All three options give {{companyName}} access to factory-built, government-backed BTR communities at a cost basis 40-50% below conventional construction.

Which structure is closest to how you typically deploy? Happy to tailor a proposal.

Colby
Tikkun Holdings

variants.subject: Florida BTR — the supply/demand gap is massive
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

One more data point for {{companyName}} — the macro story behind our Florida pipeline.

Florida Housing Demand:
• #1 state for net domestic migration — 3 consecutive years
• 500,000+ unit housing deficit statewide
• Section 8 waitlists: 2+ years in every submarket we're building in
• Rental demand growing faster than any Sun Belt market

Supply Constraints:
• Insurance crisis has frozen conventional construction financing
• Labor shortages adding 15-20% to conventional build costs
• Tariffs increasing material costs for stick-built projects
• Very limited factory-built supply in state — Tikkun is one of the only concrete modular producers in Florida

Result:
• Massive unmet demand for affordable, quality rental housing
• Government actively incentivizing new supply (Live Local Act, LIHTC, SAIL)
• Operators who secure quality communities now will have pricing power for years

Our 1,219-unit pipeline is positioned directly in the gap between overwhelming demand and constrained supply. If {{companyName}} is expanding in Florida, this is worth a conversation.

Colby
Tikkun Holdings

variants.subject: Last note from Tikkun
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

This will be my last note — I want to be respectful of your inbox.

The short version:
• 1,219 factory-built BTR units across 4 Florida projects
• Concrete construction — hurricane-rated, fire-resistant, low maintenance
• Section 8 eligible with government incentive stacking
• Looking for operating partners — management agreement, JV, or takeout structure
• Factory capacity of 2,000 units/year for ongoing pipeline

If the timing or structure doesn't work for {{companyName}} right now, no hard feelings. But if you're ever looking at Florida BTR, I hope you'll think of Tikkun.

Wishing you a great rest of the quarter.

Colby
Tikkun Holdings

items.name: Attorney Intake — Watkins v Schulz Fraud Case

variants.subject: Real Estate Fraud Case — $727K — Scottsdale, AZ — Seeking Representation
variants.body:
Dear {{firstName}},

My name is Colby Watkins. I am reaching out regarding a real estate fraud case involving $727,000 in documented losses across two properties. I am seeking experienced litigation counsel in Maricopa County.

CASE SUMMARY

Defendant: Maximilian Ryan Schulz (San Diego, CA)
Venue: Maricopa County Superior Court
Total Losses: $727,000 ($475,000 Coolidge property + $252,000 Tulum, MX property)
Additional Victims: My mother and brother invested $150,000 — three total victims

What Happened:
Between 2023 and 2024, I sent Max Schulz $727,000 for co-ownership of a property at 7549 E Coolidge St, Scottsdale, AZ 85260, and a property in Tulum, Mexico. Max represented that he owned or controlled these properties and that my investment would secure ownership. In reality:

• Max never held title to the Coolidge property
• Max spent the funds on personal expenses
• Max never delivered any ownership interest
• Max offered a $650,000 buyout in January 2026 — never paid
• The Coolidge property is encumbered by $2.55M in liens and is underwater

CLAIMS

• Fraud / Fraudulent Misrepresentation (A.R.S. § 12-543)
• Breach of Contract
• Breach of Fiduciary Duty (Max and I are 50/50 members of CWMS LLC)
• Conversion
• Unjust Enrichment
• Constructive Trust
• Punitive Damages (three separate victims — "evil mind" standard)

EVIDENCE ON HAND

• 29,000+ text messages with extensive admissions from Max
• Signed Operating Agreement (Miller Legacy Homes LLC — CWMS LLC 51% / Jake Miller 49%)
• Signed Addendum with Max's wet signature (dated 2/16/2026)
• DocuSign Property Ownership Agreement and MOU (signed by Max)
• $650,000 buyout offer email (January 15, 2026 — never honored)
• 26 documented bank transactions totaling $348,972
• Wire transfer receipts from two additional family member victims ($75,000 each)
• Fresh text admission from Max (March 2, 2026) confirming $252,000 each into Tulum
• Three promissory notes: Max → Jake Miller, $450,000 total (August 2025)

DEFENDANT'S ASSETS (recovery target)

• Primary residence: San Diego, CA — est. $1.94M
• Investment property: Las Vegas, NV — est. $848K (~$355K equity)
• Employment: VP of Sales, Kent H. Landsberg Co. (steady income)
• Total estimated assets: $3M+
• Fraud debts are non-dischargeable in bankruptcy under 11 U.S.C. § 523(a)(2)

URGENT ACTIONS NEEDED

1. Pre-judgment attachment / TRO on Max's assets (risk of dissipation)
2. Lis pendens on Coolidge property
3. Multi-state judgment enforcement: AZ filing → domesticate to CA and NV

I am based in St. Pete Beach, Florida. I operate CEW Capital Holdings Group. I have a complete evidence package organized and ready to provide, including a detailed case overview, financial records with confirmation numbers, and a full text message archive.

I am open to contingency, hybrid, or hourly fee arrangements and am prepared to move quickly.

Please let me know if you are available for an initial consultation this week.

Thank you for your time.

Best regards,
Colby Watkins
colby@wherezhemp.com
CEW Capital Holdings Group
